topic.what: High-order fully-shared masking for MQOM v2.1 signing
topic.how: Baseline masked signing design with formal security proof and optional Rijndael LUT-based acceleration for online latency reduction
topic.why_matters: MQOM is NIST additional-round MPC/TCitH candidate requiring side-channel protection; previous Picnic implementations showed such schemes are vulnerable to DPA without proper masking

characteristics.what_it_measures: Highest order of provable security against probing attacks
characteristics.evidence: Formal proof in probing leakage model
characteristics.context: Security proven in standard probing leakage model

characteristics.what_it_measures: Side-channel leakage detection via statistical testing
characteristics.evidence: Comprehensive TVLA campaign on critical small gadgets and composite gadgets

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of MQOM variants implemented and benchmarked
characteristics.evidence: All GF(2), GF(16), GF(256) variants implemented
characteristics.context: Full coverage of MQOM v2.1 parameter space

characteristics.what_itasures: Time for signing operation with masking active
Extra memory required for masking and LUT acceleration
characteristics.evidence: LUT mode incurs higher offline time and memory
characteristics.context: Tradeoff: offline precomputation vs online latency
MQOM v2.1 supports multiple field configurations

good_for: Secure deployment of MQOM in power-constrained or physically accessible environments
High-security applications requiring provable side-channel resistance
Systems needing to balance online latency vs offline precomputation time
not_for: Ultra-low memory embedded systems without LUT storage capacity
Single-cycle timing critical applications precluding offline precomputation
Scenarios only requiring security against mathematical cryptanalysis
requires: Side-channel evaluation tools (oscilloscope, power analysis setup)

novelty: First high-order fully-shared masking for MQOM v2.1
Complete formal security proof for masked design
Optional LUT-based acceleration decoupling offline precomputation from online signing
Full TVLA validation and 36-variant benchmark across GF(2), GF(16), GF(256)
open_problems: Efficiency optimization for specific field variants
Extension to higher masking orders with reduced overhead
Protection against fault injection attacks on LUT mode
Integration with additional NIST TCitH candidates (PERK, RYDE, SDitH)
